The Pincushion Cactus is a type of cactus that belongs to the Cactaceae family, characterized by its dense, spherical growth habit and short, fine spines. 

The Pincushion Cactus has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. 

The bioactive constituents of the Pincushion Cactus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. 

The most common herbal preparations made with the Pincushion Cactus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
